**The HULIKA Project**

Submitted by: Chung, Josh Matthew & Guerra, Renee Angeli  

Home security is a critical concern for homeowners around the world. Despite locked doors and windows, intruders often find a way to bypass physical barriers undetected, leading to theft, property damage, and even endangerment of lives. In the Philippines, robbery accounts for 17.7% of the national index crime volume, with many incidents occurring at night when residents and security personnel are least alert. Traditional security measures—such as manual patrols or static alarms—frequently fail to provide real-time alerts, delaying emergency response and increasing vulnerability.

This project addresses this problem by developing a Smart Intruder Detection System that:

1. Detects unauthorized movement in areas using motion sensors.  
2. Triggers instant alerts to homeowners and security personnel via software application.  
3. Logs reports on robbery attempts.

By automating surveillance and improving response times, the system aims to deter crimes and enhance safety in residential areas.

For this project, you are required to use arrays, structures, strings, and text files. There are three kinds of users – the Admin, the Guest, and the Security.


**Program Deliverables:**

| File Name | Type | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| MP-STRUCTS.h | Header file | Contains the structure declarations and function prototypes |
| main.c | C source file | Contains the main function of the program |
| Admin.c | C source file | Contains all the functions usable by the admin |
| Guest.c | C source file | Contains all the functions usable by the guest |
| Security.c | C source file | Contains all the functions usable by the security guards |
| sim.c  | C source file | Contains the functions for security simulation and reports |
| address.txt | Text file | Contains the home address information Format for text reading: \<block\>\<space\>\<lot\>\<space\>\<street\>\<space\>\<residence\>\<space\>\<barangay\> |
| admins.txt | Text file | Contains the information stored in the admin array of structure Format: \<admin1\>\<space\>\<password\>\<space\>\<contact number\> \<admin1\>\<space\>\<password\>\<space\>\<contact number\> \<EOF\> |
| adminsout.txt | Text file | Contains the formatted output based on the admins.txt file Format: List of Admins: \--------------------------------------------------------- ADMIN \#1 USERNAME: \<admin1\> Contact: \<contact number\>	 \--------------------------------------------------------- ADMIN \#2 USERNAME: \<admin2\> Contact: \<contact number\>	 \--------------------------------------------------------- \<EOF\> |
| guards.txt | Text file | Contains the information stored in the guards array of structure Format: \<guard1\>\<space\>\<password\> \<guard2\>\<space\>\<password\> . . \<EOF\> |
| guardsout.txt | Text file | Formatted output of the file guards.txt List of Guards: \--------------------------------------------------------- Guard \#1	\<guard1\> \--------------------------------------------------------- . . . \<EOF\> |
| guests.txt | Text file | Contains the guest account information stored in the guest array of structure \<guest1\>\<space\>\<password\> \<guest2\>\<space\>\<password\> . . . \<EOF\> |
| guestsout.txt | Text file | Formatted output of the guest array of structure. List of Guests: \--------------------------------------------------------- Guest \#1	\<guest1\> \--------------------------------------------------------- . . . \<EOF\> |
| security\_log.txt | Text file | Records the most recent security report during runtime. Format:  \=== SECURITY REPORT \=== Report Date: \<year\>-\<month\>-\<day\> Report Time: \<hour\>:\<minute\> \--------------------------------- TIME     | LOCATION          | PANIC BUTTON \---------|-------------------|------------- \<hour\>:\<minute\>    | \<location\>        | \<status\> \=================================== \<EOF\> |
| security\_history.txt | Text file | Where all recorded security reports are stored. \=== SECURITY REPORT \=== Report Date: \<year\>-\<month\>-\<day\> Report Time: \<hour\>:\<minute\> \--------------------------------- TIME     | LOCATION          | PANIC BUTTON \---------|-------------------|------------- \<hour\>:\<minute\>    | \<location\>        | \<status\> \=================================== \=== SECURITY REPORT \=== Report Date: \<year\>-\<month\>-\<day\> Report Time: \<hour\>:\<minute\> \--------------------------------- TIME     | LOCATION          | PANIC BUTTON \---------|-------------------|------------- \<hour\>:\<minute\>    | \<location\>        | \<status\> \=================================== . . . . \<EOF\> |

**How The Program Works:**

The program starts by displaying the login menu.  
**Admin Login**  
The login credentials are based on the data saved in the text file.  
![][image2]  
**Main Admin Menu**  
![][image3]

1. **Accounts Manager**  
   This function is responsible for managing the user accounts in the security system. It is only exclusive to the admins. In here, they can add or delete accounts, and change their password. Moreover, they can also choose to edit the home address.

![][image4]  
**1.1 Display All Accounts**  
This section displays all account types with corresponding account details.  
![][image5]  
**1.2 Change Password**  
This allows the admin to change the password of their account.  
![][image6]  
**1.3 Edit Address**  
This allows the admin to edit the home address.  
![][image7]

**1.4 Add Account**  
Adds an account based on the type specified by the admin.  
![][image8]  
	  
**1.5 Delete Account**  
Deletes an account based on the username and password given by the admin.  
![][image9]

2. **Simulate Security Event**  
   This prompts when the sensors have been configured by the admin. Here, the user can simulate a trigger of the chosen sensor and generate a report.  
   In the first iteration of the program, the admin cannot simulate whether any of the sensors have not been activated.

![][image10]  
	Once the admin configures the sensors, they can record when the sensor was triggered.  
![][image11]  
The panic button is a confirmation that the sensor was triggered. Once confirmed, the program will ask for the date and time of the event. Otherwise, it will redirect the user to the menu.  
![][image12]![][image13]

3. **Sensor Control Panel**  
   This is exclusive to the admin accounts only. In here, sensor statuses are modified to be either on or off.

![][image14]  
	**3.1 Display Sensor Setup**  
	Displays the activation status of all sensors in the household.  
![][image15]  
	**3.2 Configure Sensor Status**  
This page allows the admin to activate or deactivate the sensors. They can choose to activate all at once or configure them individually.

This is when sensors are activated all at once.  
![][image16]  
	This is the manual sensor configuration.  
![][image17]

4. **View Reports**  
   This reviews the logs of security breach reports in the household. It contains the date, time, location, and the panic button status.

![][image18]

5. **Logout**  
   Logs the admin user out, triggering another login prompt.

![][image19]  
**Guest Login**  
Logs the guest in based on their given credentials.  
![][image20]  
**Guest Menu**

1. **Record Security Simulation**  
   Allows the user to press the panic button when they are in danger. This is similar to the security simulation feature in the admin, the only difference is that they cannot configure the sensors themselves. This page is only accessible once the admin has first configured the sensors.

![][image21]  
If the admin configures the sensors, the guest can now use the feature.  
![][image22]

2. **View Reports**  
   Similar to the admin, it contains the generated reports throughout the iterations of the program.  
3. **Edit Password**  
   Allows the guest to edit their password once the program finds that their account exists.

![][image23]

4. **Logout**  
   Logs the guest user out, redirecting the page to the login prompt..

![][image24]  
**Security Login**  
![][image25]

1. **Security Menu**

**![][image26]**  
**1.1 View Reports**  
View the reports made by the admins and guests.  
![][image27]  
**1.2 Change Password**  
Allows the guard to change their password.  
![][image28]  
**1.3 Logout**  
Logs the security guard out of their account, triggering the login prompt.
